obligations.reason: Visible scope has external JTAG/TAP signals, jtag_key, pass_chk, DMI request/response handshakes, and debug-module control outputs. Need to verify the authentication signal actually mediates all DMI operations, not only a subset or unused path.
obligations.subject: JTAG/DMI external debug requester
obligations.operation: read/write DMI registers, issue debug commands, trigger halt/resume/reset/system-bus access
obligations.object: debug module control/status registers and downstream debug actions
obligations.expected_guard: JTAG/DMI accesses that can issue debug module commands or system bus accesses should be gated by explicit authentication/authorization, e.g. jtag_key/pass_chk and/or a debug enable/lifecycle condition, before dmi_req_valid_o reaches dm_csrs.
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG TAP -> dmi_jtag -> dm_top/dm_csrs DMI request path
state_conditions_to_check: reset state of pass_chk and DMI request registers
whether DMI access is possible before key check succeeds
required_evidence_slots: subject: external JTAG/TAP requester or SimJTAG/SimDTM source
operation: DMI read/write or command issue
observed_behavior: gating or missing gating at request-valid/update sites
path/reachability: tdi/tms/tck through TAP and CDC into dm_top/dm_csrs
knowledge_used: evidence closure: subject, operation, object, expected guard, observed path
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer identifies jtag_key and pass_chk but not their assignments/use sites; source inspection is required.

obligations.reason: dm_csrs exposes hart selection/control signals and dm_top turns these into debug requests. Check that arbitrary DMI writes cannot control unavailable/out-of-range harts or issue commands without dmactive/authorization and busy-state mediation.
obligations.subject: DMI debug requester
obligations.operation: halt, resume, select hart, execute abstract command
obligations.object: hart execution control: haltreq_o, resumereq_o, cmd_valid_o, hartsel_o, debug_req_o
obligations.expected_guard: Hart halt/resume and abstract command execution should require the selected hart to be valid/available and the debug module to be active/authorized; writes while cmdbusy should be rejected or recorded as errors.
obligations.candidate_path: dm_csrs abstract command interface -> haltreq/resumereq/cmd_valid outputs -> hart debug control
required_evidence_slots: expected_guard: dmactive, availability, hartsel bounds, cmdbusy handling
observed_behavior: register write decode and output generation
obligations.uncertainty: Exact register names and decode behavior need visible RTL inspection.

obligations.reason: The debug module has SBA control/data/address signals and an i_dm_sba instance. Obligations should determine whether this auxiliary bus master can access protected ROM, AES, packet, PLIC, or memory regions without the same permission checks as the CPU.
obligations.subject: debug module SBA requester
obligations.operation: read/write memory or peripherals through system bus access
obligations.object: system bus address space reachable via sbaddress/sbdata and AXI master interface
obligations.expected_guard: Debug system bus access should be constrained by debug authorization and by address/range/security-attribute checks appropriate for protected memory/peripheral regions; invalid sizes/addresses and busy updates should be blocked or signaled.
obligations.candidate_path: dm_csrs system-bus-access CSRs -> dm_sba instance -> AXI master/slave fabric
state_conditions_to_check: sbbusy handling when sbaddress or sbdata is updated
dmactive or DMI reset interaction with in-flight transactions
required_evidence_slots: expected_guard: debug auth plus address/range/PMP-like mediation
observed_behavior: address decode and error handling
path/reachability: sbaddress/sbdata to AXI fabric/peripheral mapping
obligations.uncertainty: dm_sba module body is instantiated but not listed as a visible file; inspect visible connection-level guards and downstream address map.

obligations.reason: csr_regfile exposes csr_addr_i, csr_wdata_i, csr_rdata_o, csr_we, read_access_exception, umode_i, debug_mode_q, and many protected CSR state registers. Verify decode does not allow unprivileged or non-debug accesses to privileged/debug-only state.
obligations.subject: CPU CSR interface requester
obligations.object: privileged CSRs: dpc/dscratch*, mtvec/medeleg/mideleg/mie/mip/mscratch/mepc/mcause/mtval, stvec/sscratch/sepc/scause/stval, satp/status fields, cache/perf controls
obligations.expected_guard: CSR reads/writes to privileged or debug CSRs should be checked against current privilege/debug state and read/write legality, including umode_i and debug_mode_q/dret conditions; illegal access should raise exceptions and not update protected registers.
obligations.candidate_path: csr_regfile CSR read/write decode for machine/supervisor/debug CSRs
state_conditions_to_check: csr_we derivation from fu_op and illegal access
debug CSR access only in debug mode where required
required_evidence_slots: subject: executing instruction privilege indicated by umode_i/debug_mode_q
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer does not include privilege-mode signals beyond umode_i; inspect code for additional mode state.

obligations.reason: csr_regfile contains debug-mode state and debug CSRs while dm_top provides debug_req_o. Check for correct mediation and lifecycle transitions, especially whether debug CSRs are accessible or state changes occur outside debug mode.
obligations.subject: debug module and CPU CSR requester
obligations.operation: enter debug, write/read debug CSRs, execute dret
obligations.object: debug mode state, debug PC, dpc_q, dscratch0_q, dscratch1_q, halt_csr_o/set_debug_pc_o/debug_mode_o
obligations.expected_guard: Entering debug mode should require authorized debug request and precise state capture; exiting with dret should be permitted only when in debug mode and should restore/control PC without exposing or corrupting privileged state.
obligations.candidate_path: csr_regfile debug-mode entry/exit path: debug_req_i, dpc_q, dscratch*_q, dret, set_debug_pc_o
state_conditions_to_check: dret only when debug_mode_q is set
whether dscratch/dpc are readable/writable outside debug mode
required_evidence_slots: subject: debug request source and CPU CSR instruction path
operation: debug entry/exit and debug CSR access
expected_guard: authorized debug request plus debug_mode gating for dret/debug CSR operations
observed_behavior: state updates and read mux behavior
obligations.uncertainty: Authorization may be upstream of csr_regfile; verify whether csr_regfile assumes debug_req_i is already trusted.

obligations.reason: The CSR file directly drives translation enable, load/store translation, ASID/PPN, SUM/MXR, TVM/TW/TSR outputs. Misgated CSR writes can change memory access policy for the core.
obligations.subject: CPU CSR interface requester
obligations.operation: configure address translation and privilege policy
obligations.object: SATP/status-derived translation controls and supervisor trap policy bits
obligations.expected_guard: Writes or transitions that alter address-translation and privilege policy should be restricted to the required privilege level; supervisor/user attempts should be blocked according to mode and status bits, and outputs should reflect safe reset/default states.
obligations.candidate_path: csr_regfile virtual memory and privilege policy controls: satp_ppn_o/asid_o/en_translation_o/en_ld_st_translation_o/sum_o/mxr_o/tvm_o/tw_o/tsr_o
required_evidence_slots: expected_guard: machine/supervisor privilege checks and status-bit constraints
observed_behavior: write decode and output assignment
obligations.uncertainty: Need source to distinguish actual mode encodings and write masks.

obligations.reason: Fact layer shows APB-to-reg bridges for packet and AES, a rom2 instance with pkey_loc_o/jtag_key relation, and multiple peripherals. Check whether bus accesses are distinguished by requester/privilege and whether protected assets are readable/writable through generic MMIO paths.
obligations.subject: AXI/APB bus requester
obligations.object: APB/AXI peripheral registers and data outputs, especially AES and key/ROM related signals
obligations.expected_guard: MMIO access to security-sensitive peripherals such as AES/key/packet/ROM2 should be mediated by address decode plus privilege/security attributes or explicit local guards; read data muxes should not expose secrets unless authorized.
obligations.candidate_path: APB/AXI peripheral access in ariane_peripherals: plic, rom2, packet, AES, UART/SPI/Ethernet register mappings
state_conditions_to_check: read data defaults for unmapped/unauthorized accesses
debug SBA reachability to same MMIO space
required_evidence_slots: subject: AXI/APB bus master (CPU or debug SBA)
expected_guard: address decode plus privilege/security/local access guard
obligations.uncertainty: Some instantiated peripheral module bodies are not listed; focus on visible wrappers and connections.

obligations.reason: Visible signals include rom2_we, rom2_wdata, rom2_rdata, pkey_loc_o, a jtag_key output in peripherals, and jtag_key input to dmi_jtag. Verify ROM2 cannot be overwritten by ordinary bus writes and that key material is not exposed through readable data paths.
obligations.subject: AXI/APB bus requester and debug authentication path
obligations.operation: write/read ROM2 and route key material to JTAG authentication
obligations.expected_guard: ROM-like or key-location storage should reject writes or require a provisioning/privileged guard; any key or key-derived value routed to jtag_key or bus read data should have explicit authorization and safe reset semantics.
obligations.candidate_path: rom2 module and ariane_peripherals ROM2/key wiring
state_conditions_to_check: whether we_i is ignored or updates storage
whether pkey_loc_o/jtag_key is externally or MMIO readable
required_evidence_slots: subject: bus requester and JTAG auth consumer
operation: ROM2 write/read and key routing
expected_guard: write-protect/provisioning guard and read authorization
observed_behavior: we_i handling and rdata/key assignments
obligations.uncertainty: ROM2 is only 50 lines, so exact storage/write behavior must be checked directly.

obligations.reason: dmi_jtag_tap has testmode_i, a DFT TCK mux, and DMI select/access signals. Need to ensure test mode changes only clocking/test behavior and does not bypass authentication or enable protected DMI operations by itself.
obligations.subject: test/JTAG interface requester
obligations.operation: shift/update/capture DMI or select debug instructions under test mode
obligations.object: TAP instruction/data registers, dmi_access_o, dmi_tdi_o/dmi_tdo_i, tck clock path
obligations.expected_guard: testmode_i/DFT clock muxing should not create an unauthenticated path to shift/update DMI registers or force TAP state transitions that bypass jtag_key/pass_chk authorization.
obligations.candidate_path: dmi_jtag_tap testmode_i and DFT clock mux path
required_evidence_slots: operation: TAP shift/update/capture and DMI select
object: DMI access signals and TAP state
expected_guard: debug authorization remains enforced independent of testmode_i
observed_behavior: mux/select logic and downstream gating
obligations.uncertainty: testmode source/trust boundary may be outside visible scope.

obligations.reason: dm_csrs has explicit reset-control outputs and reset-state tracking. Check whether reset controls are protected and whether deactivation/reset clears sensitive command/SBA state to prevent stale or unauthorized operations after reset transitions.
obligations.subject: DMI debug requester
obligations.operation: assert/deassert non-debug reset, activate/deactivate debug module, observe reset status
obligations.object: ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, havereset_d, debug module internal state
obligations.expected_guard: Non-debug-module reset and dmactive changes should be allowed only through authorized debug writes and should safely clear/retain state according to spec; reset requests must not be asserted by unauthenticated or stale DMI state.
obligations.candidate_path: dm_csrs reset/control registers: dmactive_o, ndmreset_o, dmi_rst_ni, havereset_d
state_conditions_to_check: dmactive_o reset default and clear behavior
whether sbaddress/sbdata/command state is cleared on dmactive deassertion
required_evidence_slots: expected_guard: authorized DMI write and safe lifecycle clearing
observed_behavior: reset assignment and state retention
obligations.uncertainty: Need to inspect sequential always blocks for exact reset domains.
